description: A setup that silently relies on a globally-installed tool or env var present only on the original author's machine.
body: Failure mode: bootstrap appears to work for the author but fails on a fresh machine because it assumes a global CLI, a specific runtime version, or an env var that was never documented or provisioned. Detection signals: fresh-clone setup fails with 'command not found' or missing-config errors; steps work for one person only; no prerequisite check. Do not assume the author's environment on a new machine. Safer fallback: have bootstrap check and report missing prerequisites with install guidance, pin tool versions, and document required env vars with safe defaults. Risk boundary: never auto-install or default a real secret to make bootstrap pass; require the human to supply credentials deliberately.
